title: A beautiful dinner at Lakeview Palace in Macau, matching Chinese food with some top Chinese wines
summary: Website: https://www.wynnresortsmacau.com/en/wynn-palace/dining/lakeview-palace I arrived tonight in Macau in advance of judging the Wynn Signature Chinese Wine Awards. We dined at
content:
Website: https://www.wynnresortsmacau.com/en/wynn-palace/dining/lakeview-palace

I arrived tonight in Macau in advance of judging the Wynn Signature Chinese Wine Awards. We dined at the Lakeview Palace, one of several high-end restaurants in Wynn Palace, and as well as a really good set menu we had some great pairings with Chinese wines.

Tiansai Skyline of Gobi Sparkling Wine 2017 Xinjiang, China
Some toasty richness here as well as bright lemony fruit. Zesty with some freshness, and subtle bread and toast richness. Stylish sparkling wine with real quality. 93/100

Chateau Nine Peaks Qi Chardonnay 2021 Shandong, China
Delicate, elegant lemony and stony with nice texture. This is on the lighter side but it’s very pretty with nice balance, showing purity and focus. 93/100

Domaine Franco-Chinois Reserve 2019 Hebei, China (magnum)
Classy, gravelly, grainy with sweet blackcurrant fruit and some nice graininess. Grippy but with lovely fruit sweetness. Such balance and poise here with real finesse. 94/100

Sacred Snow Mountain Hua Luo Xiang Yun Ice Wine 2023 Yunnan, China
This is made from Vidal. Concentrated and rich with apricot and lemon as well as grapey richness. Lovely density and finishes spicy. Beautiful. 94 /100

title: Highlights: Renyiyuan Tao Cabernet Sauvignon 2020 Ningxia, China
summary: This is one of the best Chinese wines I’ve tasted. It’s from Renyiyuan Vineyard, which was established in 2010 in
content:
This is one of the best Chinese wines I’ve tasted. It’s from Renyiyuan Vineyard, which was established in 2010 in the Jinshan sub-region of the eastern foothills of the Helan Mountain in the Ningxia region. It’s bold and rich but perfectly balanced. It won best overall red wine in the 2025 edition of the Wynn Signature Chinese Wine Awards that I judged in last year.

Renyiyuan Tao Cabernet Sauvignon 2020 Ninxia, China
15% alcohol. Lovely aromatics of brooding blackcurrant fruit with some sweet cherry and fine spices. In the mouth this is dense and structured with great concentration to the cherry and blackcurrant fruit, showing fruit sweetness but also firm tannins. It’s primary and intense with a long future ahead of it. Really good. 95/100

title: Another airline lounge raid, 12 wines tried
summary: Flying out to Hong Kong today so I did a lounge raid (T5 BA first class lounge – because of
content:
Flying out to Hong Kong today so I did a lounge raid (T5 BA first class lounge – because of changes to the BA executive club qualifying rules, this is definitely the last time I’ll be in here: I mainly fly economy so for me to get Gold status was something of a miracle). 12 wines, and good to see English fizz being shown off to the world. But if this is the first class lounge, it’s a sorry state of play to be offering many of these wines that would have struggled to make business class standard a couple of decades back. I’m sure that First passengers get better wines on board. But business class selections, almost universally, are budget these days. It’s a shame for wine. Rant over.

I think this was my biggest lounge raid to date, but just tiny sips!

Planeta Alastro 2024 Menfi, Sicilia, Italy
Vivid and citrusy with some pithy edges. Lively and very fruity. 90/100

Clos Henri Sauvignon Blanc 2023 Marlborough, New Zealand
Real intensity. Lime and elderflower with a herby edge. Purecand quite mineral with high acid. 93/100

Donnofugata Vigna di Galvi 2023 Sicilia, Italy
Juicy peachy and intense with a hint of dairy and nice juiciness. Fresh and rich at the same time. 90/100

Martinborough Te Tera Pinot Noir 2022 Martinborough, New Zealand
Floral cherry fruit nose. Juicy, herby, tangy palate is quite pretty. Some secondary savouriness here. 91/100

Torre Mora Chiuse Vidalba Etna Rosso 2021 Sicilia, Italy
Ripe and spicy with some savoury notes as well as sweet cherry and plum fruit. Satisfying. Might have been better two years ago? 90/100

Miral Syrah 2024 Sicilia, Italy
Ripe, juicy and sweetly fruited with lovely cherry and blackberry fruit. A real crowd pleaser and nicely balanced. 89/100

Balfour Rosé de Noirs NV England
Juicy and fruity with a touch of sweetness offsetting the red cherry and pear fruit. Massive drinkability here. Stylish. 90/100

Wiston Brut NV England
Tart and lively with appealing lemon and cherry fruit with a zesty edge. Has great balance with lively fruit, keen acidity and a touch of sweetness. 90/100

Simpsons Chalklands Classic Cuvée NV England
Lovely fruit here, with flesh on the bones. Bright lemon and pear with a hint of peach, showing a lovely flow across the palate. Just a hint of toastiness, too. 91/100

Whispering Angel Rosé 2024 Côtes de Provence, France
Balanced and appealing with cherry, watermelon and pear fruit. Nice drinkability with no surprises. 88/100

Champagne Besserat de Bellefon Grande Tradition Brut Rosé NV France
Juicy and appealing with sweet cherry, mandarin and lime fruit. Brisk acidity keeps the focus. Nicely drinkable. 91/100

Champagne Aberlé 1757 Brut NV France
3/4 years on lees, 6 g/l dosage. Bright and lively with pear and peach richness and hints of toast. Nice depth here but also freshness. Very stylish. 91/100

title: Books: an impressive guide to Japanese Wine from Nick Rowan
summary: Japanese Wine: History, Regions, Wineries By Nick Rowan Wine Fogg, 2025, website https://winefogg.com/ I’ve visited Japanese wine regions several times,
content:
Japanese Wine: History, Regions, Wineries

By Nick Rowan

Wine Fogg, 2025, website https://winefogg.com/

I’ve visited Japanese wine regions several times, and I think there’s an interesting wine scene here that’s worth exploring. With few exceptions, though, everything is on a small scale, and this extremely fragmented production base, coupled with strong local demand, makes finding Japanese wines on export markets quite a challenge.

For most non-Japanese, the extent of their knowledge of Japanese wine is limited to Koshu wines from the Yamanashi region, because there’s been some effort to promote these abroad. But while Koshu can be interesting, there’s a lot more to Japanese wine than this. I’ve tried to communicate this, writing up my trips on wineanorak.com, but now there is an amazing resource available in the English language: a seriously in-depth, scholarly book from Nick Rowan.

Nick has spent a lot of time in Japan, and for a while lived there. He knows his stuff, and he’s put together an incredibly useful and fully comprehensive book that acts as a travel guide to, for those who want to visit. There are details on some 700 wineries here (this gives an idea of the fragmentation of the industry) as well as a lot of background such as grape varieties, viticulture, history and even the Japanese language. The writing style is clear and engaging, and this represents a unique and invaluable resource. Few wine countries or regions have a reference work this clear, thorough and extensive.

It’s a hefty hardback of 434 pages, in black and white, but really well put together. I imagine the market for this book will not be huge, but this isn’t the point. Few traditional publishers would even look at a project like this, and Nick’s self-publishing work is of a higher standard than that of many of the established publishers. If you have any interest in Japanese wine and are thinking of a visit, this book is more-or-less compulsory.

The book can be bought here

Or here in the USA

title: Apollo’s Praise: an exciting new producer in New York’s Finger Lakes wine region
summary: Website: https://apollospraise.com/ This is an exciting new project in the Finger Lakes of New York State. Julia Hoyle (winemaker at
content:
Website: https://apollospraise.com/

This is an exciting new project in the Finger Lakes of New York State. Julia Hoyle (winemaker at Hosmer) and Kelby Russell (who was winemaker at Red Newt) managed to buy the Lahoma vineyard in 2023 from grower Ken Fulkerson. For a long time Lahoma had been the source of many of the best wines from Red Newt.

It’s a 20 acre site, and has some special plots, most notably The Knoll, a small hill (maybe hill is too strong a word).

The name Apollo’s Praise comes from Kelby’s musical interests: in a previous life he was an orchestra manager. He performed a song called Glorious Apollo with the Harvard Glee Club as an undergraduate.

Currently the wines are made in two locations. Kelby looks after some at Sheldrake Point, while Juila looks after some at Hosmer.

Apollo’s Praise Dry Riesling Lahoma Vineyard 2024 Finger Lakes, New York
This is made by Juila. 20% neutral French oak, 80% stainless steel. Macerated for 2-3 days, then inoculated with a range of strains. This is textural, dry and balanced with some weight to the citrus fruit, showing some depth and some fruit sweetness. Textured and nicely complex. 93/100

Apollo’s Praise The Knoll Riesling 2024 Finger Lakes, New York
This is made by Kelby. 5 days on skins then pressed off for wild ferment, all in stainless steel. Wonderful aromatics here, with lively spic-laden citrus fruit and a hint of apricot. Crystalline in the mouth. Textured, fine and pretty, but with structure. Such precision and purity. 95/100

Apollo’s Praise The Knoll Riesling 2023 Finger Lakes, New York
13% alcohol. Great to try this, the first release of the Knoll, the flagship wine from Kelby Russell and Julia Hoyle’s new venture, after they bought the vineyard that Kelby had been working with at Red Newt for many years. Fruit crushed, cold soaked for 4 days, then fermented with indigenous yeasts in tank. Lovely precision and detail with lime, lemon and then some almost floral notes, with great intensity. Such elegance and precision in the mouth, combining sweet citrus fruit with fine spicy detail, tapering to a fine finish. This has power and refinement and is truly exceptional. 95/100

Apollo’s Praise Lahoma Vineyard Riesling 2024 Finger Lakes, New York
Made in a Kabinett style, 56 g/l rs, 8.7 TA, 3.04 pH, 8% alcohol. Off-dry, juicy and lively with nice pure citrus fruit as well as melony richness. Good energy to this pretty wine. 94/100

Apollo’s Praise Pentecostal Block Riesling 2024 Finger Lakes, New York
This is from another hill 50 m away from the Knoll. Barrel-fermented, made in a Spätlese style. Off dry and textural with lovely balance and nice freshness, showing good balance and nice acidity countering the sweetness. 93/100

Apollo’s Praise Grüner Veltliner 2024 Finger Lakes, New York
There’s half an acre of this planted on the back part of the Knoll. Supple, fresh and textural with trademark white pepper and crystalline citrus fruit. Such texture here. Well balanced and fine, with nice length. 94/100

Apollo’s Praise Chardonnay 2024 Finger Lakes, New York
All fermented and aged in puncheons, some new. Fresh, linear and stylish with integrated oak, subtle mealy detail, some brightness and lovely balance. Pure and textural. 94/100

Apollo’s Praise Cabernet Franc Picardie 2024 Finger Lakes, New York
This isn’t all from Lahoma. Fresh and supple with raspberry and cherry fruit. Juicy, stony and mineral, with nice weight and lovely drinkability. 92/100

Apollo’s Praise Gamay Noir Lahoma 2024 Finger Lakes, New York
Fermented and aged in a 2500 litre cask. Some reduction. Juicy and crunchy with nice bright cherry and berry fruit, showing purity and freshness, finishimng bright with good energy. 93/100

title: Porto in pictures, with a short film
summary: Just back from a few days in Porto. While I was there I had a chance to wander around. Some
content:
Just back from a few days in Porto. While I was there I had a chance to wander around. Some pictures and a film.

A film:

title: Augalevada, a star from Spain’s Ribeiro
summary: Iago Garrido founded Fazenda Augalevada back in 2008, after purchasing 4 hectares of abandoned terraces in the northern part of
content:
Iago Garrido founded Fazenda Augalevada back in 2008, after purchasing 4 hectares of abandoned terraces in the northern part of Ribeiro, which he has renovated and now farms biodynamically. He’s based in the Rioboó zone, which used to have a reputation for red wines. These wines are quite brilliant. I tasted with Iago at the Simplesmente Vinho event in Porto, February 2026.

Ollos Branco 2024 Ribeiro, Spain
65% Treixadura, 25% Agudelo (Chenin Blanc) and 10% Lado. Lively, very bright, vivid and mineral with good acidity. Taut and compressed. Lively citrus fruit drives this. 92/100

Ollos de Roque 2023 Ribeiro, Spain
Same blend as branco, aged 10 months under flor yeast in one-third 400 litre amphora and two-thirds in old oak. One parcel, granitic soils. So interesting, with notes of dried herbs and stony mineral detail. Fresh and layered. Wet stone finish. 93/100

Ollos Tinto 2024 Ribeiro, Spain
Caiño Longo, Brancellao, Espadeiro and Sousón, 70% whole cluster, barrels and amphora. This is fresh and floral but with a savoury edge. Wide dynamic range with subtle meaty depth as well as freshness. 94/100

Ollos de Maia 2023 Ribeiro, Spain
45% Caiño Longo, 45% Brancellao, and 10% Caíño da Terra, aged in larger oak, partially under flor. Lovely tension, good acidity, lively fruit. Really expressive with dried herbs and wild strawberries. 94/100

title: Eulogio Pomares and Zarate: amazing wines from Rías Baixas
summary: Website: https://www.zarate.es/ Eulogio Pomares is making some of the best wines in Rías Baixas. He works with two labels: Zarate
content:
Website: https://www.zarate.es/

Eulogio Pomares is making some of the best wines in Rías Baixas. He works with two labels: Zarate is the family property, while he also has a separate cellar where he makes wines under his own name. I tasted with Eulogio and Rebeca at Simplesmente Vinho in Porto, February 2026.

I’ve included a note on Sal da Terra, which is a collaborative project that I’m involved with, but it’s Eulogio’s wine, so I’ve scored it. But bear this conflict of interest in mind as you read.

Zarate Albariño 2025 Rías Baixas, Spain
This is really good, showing some pear, peach and lime fruit with a lovely acid line, and nice depth. Good ripeness and good acidity in tandem. 93/100

Zarate Balado 2024 Rías Baixas, Spain
Single vineyard, old vines, fermented and aged in stainless steel for six months. Rich, intense, textural. Really expressive with bold citrus and apricot notes. Seriously good. 94/100

Eulogio Pomares Castineiro 2024 Rías Baixas, Spain
From a coastal vineyard at Castrelo, this is fermented and aged in chestnut barrels and undergoes full malolactic. Powerful, showing great energy: saline, green apple, citrus drive. Real length here. 95/100

Eulogio Pomares Sal da Terra 2024 Rías Baixas, Spain
From two vineyards. Coastal, aged in chestnut; inland, aged in concrete. Richly aromatic but still fresh, powerful pear and peach and a spicy, saline undertow. Real precision. 95/100

title: Nanclares y Prieto, beautiful wines from Rías Baixas
summary: Website: https://nanclaresyprieto.es/ I tasted these wines at Simplesmente Vinho in Porto in February 2026 with Alberto Nanclares (pictured above). He
content:
Website: https://nanclaresyprieto.es/

I tasted these wines at Simplesmente Vinho in Porto in February 2026 with Alberto Nanclares (pictured above). He started this winery in the late 1990s a few years after he’d moved to Castrelo. An economist, he’d bought a house with vineyards, and then decided to make wine. After using an enologist he turned to making the wines himself, and also farming in a less interventionist way. The Prieto in the name is from Sylvia Prieto, a consultant who’d worked with Alberto and who became a partner in the project a decade ago. They make beautifully pure, expressive wines both from their own vineyards and also those of grower partners. The whites are brilliant, and while the reds are good I think a less oxidative winemaking approach might suit them better.

Nanclares y Prieto Blanco 2024 Rías Baixas, Spain
Albariño from lots of different plots, almost all in stainless steel. Nice presence: bright and pithy with good acidity under the lively citrus fruit. 92/100

Nanclares y Prieto Obocoi Vello 2023 Rías Baixas, Spain
Whole cluster foot trodden and macerated for 2 days, then pressed to chestnut barrels. Saline and dry with some pithy character. Nice salinity and weight here, showing lovely balance. Fine and characterful. 94/100

Nanclares y Prieto Alberto Nanclares 2024 Rías Baixas, Spain
This is Albariño fermented and aged in used French oak. Concentrated, stony and vivid with nice citrus presence. Saline. Dried herbs. 94/100

Nanclares y Prieto Soverribas 2024 Rías Baixas, Spain
This is from a single parcel of Albariño with more clay. Powerful and structured. Stony and mineral. Green apples and herbs. Real energy. 93/100

Nanclares y Prieto Chuvisca 2023 Rías Baixas, Spain
Foot trodden whole clusters, 6 hours’ maceration. Pale, very stony and mineral with nice texture. Some cherry and pear. 92/100

Nanclares y Prieto Tinto 2024 Rías Baixas, Spain
Mencía, Caiño, Espadeiro, Sousón and Brancellao from a collection of 8 plots in the villages of Salnés and Condado zones. Some lift on the nose. Restrained, mineral, taut and savoury on the palate. Juicy. 92/100

Nanclares y Prieto Vermella 2021 Rías Baixas, Spain
Caiño and Mencía from several parcels in Salnes. Savory herbal and bright with a mineral streak. Distinctive. High acid. 91/100

Nanclares y Prieto Oson Das Pisadas 2022 Rías Baixas, Spain
Mencia. Savoury, stony. Well balanced with restrained fruit. Has nice energy. 93/100

UK agent: https://www.indigowine.com/producer/alberto-nanclares/

title: Highlights: two vintages of the Blanc de Raisins Blanc from Mas de Daumas Gassac, Languedoc star
summary: Two versions of the same wine, eight years apart. Mas de Daumas Gassac are best known for their red wine,
content:
Two versions of the same wine, eight years apart. Mas de Daumas Gassac are best known for their red wine, but they grow a lot of interesting white varieties, and this is their top white, blending together an interesting mix of grapes. This ages so well.

Mas de Daumas Gassac Blanc de Raisins Blanc 2024 Languedoc, France
13.5% alcohol. 35% Viognier, 29% Chardonnay, 13% Petit Manseng, 10% Chenin, 6% Muscat, 7% others. Aromatic stone fruits with a hint of mint and dried herbs. The palate has aniseed, pear and white peach, as well as nice texture. So expressive, with some volume. 94/100

Mas de Daumas Gassac Blanc de Raisins Blanc 2016 Languedoc, France
14% alcohol. 27% Viognier, 25% Petit Manseng, 21% Chardonnay, 13% Chenin, 14% others. Bold and powerful. Richly textured with peach, pear and apricot fruit as well as some toast and spice notes and a lovely mineral dimension: it’s a sort of sweet textural minerality, finishing with mandarin and sea salt. This shows some evolution, but not as much as you’d think. Very stylish. 94/100

title: A beautiful dinner at Lakeview Palace in Macau, matching Chinese food with some top Chinese wines
summary: Website: https://www.wynnresortsmacau.com/en/wynn-palace/dining/lakeview-palace I arrived tonight in Macau in advance of judging the Wynn Signature Chinese Wine Awards. We dined at
content:
Website: https://www.wynnresortsmacau.com/en/wynn-palace/dining/lakeview-palace

title: Highlights: Renyiyuan Tao Cabernet Sauvignon 2020 Ningxia, China
summary: This is one of the best Chinese wines I’ve tasted. It’s from Renyiyuan Vineyard, which was established in 2010 in
content:
This is one of the best Chinese wines I’ve tasted. It’s from Renyiyuan Vineyard, which was established in 2010 in the Jinshan sub-region of the eastern foothills of the Helan Mountain in the Ningxia region. It’s bold and rich but perfectly balanced. title: Another airline lounge raid, 12 wines tried
summary: Flying out to Hong Kong today so I did a lounge raid (T5 BA first class lounge – because of
content:
Flying out to Hong Kong today so I did a lounge raid (T5 BA first class lounge – because of changes to the BA executive club qualifying rules, this is definitely the last time I’ll be in here: I mainly fly economy so for me to get Gold status was something of a miracle). 12 wines, and good to see English fizz being shown off to the world. But if this is the first class lounge, it’s a sorry state of play to be offering many of these wines that would have struggled to make business class standard a couple of decades back. I’m sure that First passengers get better wines on board. But business class selections, almost universally, are budget these days. It’s a shame for wine. Rant over.

I think this was my biggest lounge raid to date, but just tiny sips!

Planeta Alastro 2024 Menfi, Sicilia, Italy
Vivid and citrusy with some pithy edges. Lively and very fruity. 90/100

Clos Henri Sauvignon Blanc 2023 Marlborough, New Zealand
Real intensity. Lime and elderflower with a herby edge. Purecand quite mineral with high acid. 93/100

Donnofugata Vigna di Galvi 2023 Sicilia, Italy
Juicy peachy and intense with a hint of dairy and nice juiciness. Fresh and rich at the same time. 90/100

Martinborough Te Tera Pinot Noir 2022 Martinborough, New Zealand
Floral cherry fruit nose. Juicy, herby, tangy palate is quite pretty. Some secondary savouriness here. 91/100

Torre Mora Chiuse Vidalba Etna Rosso 2021 Sicilia, Italy
Ripe and spicy with some savoury notes as well as sweet cherry and plum fruit. Satisfying. Might have been better two years ago? 90/100

Miral Syrah 2024 Sicilia, Italy
Ripe, juicy and sweetly fruited with lovely cherry and blackberry fruit. A real crowd pleaser and nicely balanced. 89/100

Balfour Rosé de Noirs NV England
Juicy and fruity with a touch of sweetness offsetting the red cherry and pear fruit. Massive drinkability here. Stylish. 90/100

Wiston Brut NV England
Tart and lively with appealing lemon and cherry fruit with a zesty edge. Has great balance with lively fruit, keen acidity and a touch of sweetness. 90/100

Simpsons Chalklands Classic Cuvée NV England
Lovely fruit here, with flesh on the bones. Bright lemon and pear with a hint of peach, showing a lovely flow across the palate. Just a hint of toastiness, too. 91/100

Whispering Angel Rosé 2024 Côtes de Provence, France
Balanced and appealing with cherry, watermelon and pear fruit. Nice drinkability with no surprises. 88/100

Champagne Besserat de Bellefon Grande Tradition Brut Rosé NV France
Juicy and appealing with sweet cherry, mandarin and lime fruit. Brisk acidity keeps the focus. Nicely drinkable. 91/100

Champagne Aberlé 1757 Brut NV France
3/4 years on lees, 6 g/l dosage. Bright and lively with pear and peach richness and hints of toast. Nice depth here but also freshness. Very stylish. 91/100

title: Books: an impressive guide to Japanese Wine from Nick Rowan
summary: Japanese Wine: History, Regions, Wineries By Nick Rowan Wine Fogg, 2025, website https://winefogg.com/ I’ve visited Japanese wine regions several times,
content:
Japanese Wine: History, Regions, Wineries

By Nick Rowan

Wine Fogg, 2025, website https://winefogg.com/

I’ve visited Japanese wine regions several times, and I think there’s an interesting wine scene here that’s worth exploring. With few exceptions, though, everything is on a small scale, and this extremely fragmented production base, coupled with strong local demand, makes finding Japanese wines on export markets quite a challenge.

Nick has spent a lot of time in Japan, and for a while lived there. title: Apollo’s Praise: an exciting new producer in New York’s Finger Lakes wine region
summary: Website: https://apollospraise.com/ This is an exciting new project in the Finger Lakes of New York State. Julia Hoyle (winemaker at
content:
Website: https://apollospraise.com/

This is an exciting new project in the Finger Lakes of New York State. Julia Hoyle (winemaker at Hosmer) and Kelby Russell (who was winemaker at Red Newt) managed to buy the Lahoma vineyard in 2023 from grower Ken Fulkerson. For a long time Lahoma had been the source of many of the best wines from Red Newt.

It’s a 20 acre site, and has some special plots, most notably The Knoll, a small hill (maybe hill is too strong a word).

The name Apollo’s Praise comes from Kelby’s musical interests: in a previous life he was an orchestra manager. He performed a song called Glorious Apollo with the Harvard Glee Club as an undergraduate.

Currently the wines are made in two locations. Kelby looks after some at Sheldrake Point, while Juila looks after some at Hosmer.

This is made by Juila. 20% neutral French oak, 80% stainless steel. Macerated for 2-3 days, then inoculated with a range of strains. This is textural, dry and balanced with some weight to the citrus fruit, showing some depth and some fruit sweetness. Textured and nicely complex. 93/100

Apollo’s Praise The Knoll Riesling 2023 Finger Lakes, New York
13% alcohol. Great to try this, the first release of the Knoll, the flagship wine from Kelby Russell and Julia Hoyle’s new venture, after they bought the vineyard that Kelby had been working with at Red Newt for many years. Fruit crushed, cold soaked for 4 days, then fermented with indigenous yeasts in tank. Lovely precision and detail with lime, lemon and then some almost floral notes, with great intensity. Such elegance and precision in the mouth, combining sweet citrus fruit with fine spicy detail, tapering to a fine finish. This has power and refinement and is truly exceptional. 95/100

Apollo’s Praise Lahoma Vineyard Riesling 2024 Finger Lakes, New York

Apollo’s Praise Pentecostal Block Riesling 2024 Finger Lakes, New York
This is from another hill 50 m away from the Knoll. Barrel-fermented, made in a Spätlese style. Off dry and textural with lovely balance and nice freshness, showing good balance and nice acidity countering the sweetness. 93/100

Apollo’s Praise Grüner Veltliner 2024 Finger Lakes, New York
There’s half an acre of this planted on the back part of the Knoll. Supple, fresh and textural with trademark white pepper and crystalline citrus fruit. Such texture here. Well balanced and fine, with nice length. 94/100

Apollo’s Praise Chardonnay 2024 Finger Lakes, New York
All fermented and aged in puncheons, some new. Fresh, linear and stylish with integrated oak, subtle mealy detail, some brightness and lovely balance. Pure and textural. 94/100

Apollo’s Praise Cabernet Franc Picardie 2024 Finger Lakes, New York
This isn’t all from Lahoma. Fresh and supple with raspberry and cherry fruit. Juicy, stony and mineral, with nice weight and lovely drinkability. 92/100

Apollo’s Praise Gamay Noir Lahoma 2024 Finger Lakes, New York
Fermented and aged in a 2500 litre cask. Some reduction. Juicy and crunchy with nice bright cherry and berry fruit, showing purity and freshness, finishimng bright with good energy. 93/100

title: Porto in pictures, with a short film
summary: Just back from a few days in Porto. While I was there I had a chance to wander around. Some
content:
Just back from a few days in Porto. While I was there I had a chance to wander around. Some pictures and a film.

A film:

title: Augalevada, a star from Spain’s Ribeiro
summary: Iago Garrido founded Fazenda Augalevada back in 2008, after purchasing 4 hectares of abandoned terraces in the northern part of
content:
Iago Garrido founded Fazenda Augalevada back in 2008, after purchasing 4 hectares of abandoned terraces in the northern part of Ribeiro, which he has renovated and now farms biodynamically. He’s based in the Rioboó zone, which used to have a reputation for red wines. These wines are quite brilliant. I tasted with Iago at the Simplesmente Vinho event in Porto, February 2026.

Ollos Branco 2024 Ribeiro, Spain
65% Treixadura, 25% Agudelo (Chenin Blanc) and 10% Lado. Lively, very bright, vivid and mineral with good acidity. Taut and compressed. Lively citrus fruit drives this. 92/100

Ollos de Roque 2023 Ribeiro, Spain
Same blend as branco, aged 10 months under flor yeast in one-third 400 litre amphora and two-thirds in old oak. One parcel, granitic soils. So interesting, with notes of dried herbs and stony mineral detail. Fresh and layered. Wet stone finish. 93/100

Ollos Tinto 2024 Ribeiro, Spain
Caiño Longo, Brancellao, Espadeiro and Sousón, 70% whole cluster, barrels and amphora. This is fresh and floral but with a savoury edge. Wide dynamic range with subtle meaty depth as well as freshness. 94/100

Ollos de Maia 2023 Ribeiro, Spain
45% Caiño Longo, 45% Brancellao, and 10% Caíño da Terra, aged in larger oak, partially under flor. Lovely tension, good acidity, lively fruit. Really expressive with dried herbs and wild strawberries. 94/100

title: Eulogio Pomares and Zarate: amazing wines from Rías Baixas
summary: Website: https://www.zarate.es/ Eulogio Pomares is making some of the best wines in Rías Baixas. He works with two labels: Zarate
content:
Website: https://www.zarate.es/

Eulogio Pomares is making some of the best wines in Rías Baixas. He works with two labels: Zarate is the family property, while he also has a separate cellar where he makes wines under his own name. I tasted with Eulogio and Rebeca at Simplesmente Vinho in Porto, February 2026.

I’ve included a note on Sal da Terra, which is a collaborative project that I’m involved with, but it’s Eulogio’s wine, so I’ve scored it. But bear this conflict of interest in mind as you read.

Zarate Albariño 2025 Rías Baixas, Spain
This is really good, showing some pear, peach and lime fruit with a lovely acid line, and nice depth. Good ripeness and good acidity in tandem. 93/100

Zarate Balado 2024 Rías Baixas, Spain
Single vineyard, old vines, fermented and aged in stainless steel for six months. Rich, intense, textural. Really expressive with bold citrus and apricot notes. Seriously good. 94/100

Eulogio Pomares Castineiro 2024 Rías Baixas, Spain
From a coastal vineyard at Castrelo, this is fermented and aged in chestnut barrels and undergoes full malolactic. Powerful, showing great energy: saline, green apple, citrus drive. Real length here. 95/100

Eulogio Pomares Sal da Terra 2024 Rías Baixas, Spain
From two vineyards. Coastal, aged in chestnut; inland, aged in concrete. Richly aromatic but still fresh, powerful pear and peach and a spicy, saline undertow. Real precision. 95/100

title: Nanclares y Prieto, beautiful wines from Rías Baixas
summary: Website: https://nanclaresyprieto.es/ I tasted these wines at Simplesmente Vinho in Porto in February 2026 with Alberto Nanclares (pictured above). He
content:
Website: https://nanclaresyprieto.es/

I tasted these wines at Simplesmente Vinho in Porto in February 2026 with Alberto Nanclares (pictured above). He started this winery in the late 1990s a few years after he’d moved to Castrelo. An economist, he’d bought a house with vineyards, and then decided to make wine. After using an enologist he turned to making the wines himself, and also farming in a less interventionist way. The Prieto in the name is from Sylvia Prieto, a consultant who’d worked with Alberto and who became a partner in the project a decade ago. They make beautifully pure, expressive wines both from their own vineyards and also those of grower partners. The whites are brilliant, and while the reds are good I think a less oxidative winemaking approach might suit them better.

Nanclares y Prieto Blanco 2024 Rías Baixas, Spain
Albariño from lots of different plots, almost all in stainless steel. Nice presence: bright and pithy with good acidity under the lively citrus fruit. 92/100

Nanclares y Prieto Obocoi Vello 2023 Rías Baixas, Spain
Whole cluster foot trodden and macerated for 2 days, then pressed to chestnut barrels. Saline and dry with some pithy character. Nice salinity and weight here, showing lovely balance. Fine and characterful. 94/100

Nanclares y Prieto Alberto Nanclares 2024 Rías Baixas, Spain
This is Albariño fermented and aged in used French oak. Concentrated, stony and vivid with nice citrus presence. Saline. Dried herbs. 94/100

Nanclares y Prieto Soverribas 2024 Rías Baixas, Spain
This is from a single parcel of Albariño with more clay. Powerful and structured. Stony and mineral. Green apples and herbs. Real energy. 93/100

Nanclares y Prieto Chuvisca 2023 Rías Baixas, Spain
Foot trodden whole clusters, 6 hours’ maceration. Pale, very stony and mineral with nice texture. Some cherry and pear. 92/100

Nanclares y Prieto Tinto 2024 Rías Baixas, Spain
Mencía, Caiño, Espadeiro, Sousón and Brancellao from a collection of 8 plots in the villages of Salnés and Condado zones. Some lift on the nose. Restrained, mineral, taut and savoury on the palate. Juicy. 92/100

Nanclares y Prieto Vermella 2021 Rías Baixas, Spain
Caiño and Mencía from several parcels in Salnes. Savory herbal and bright with a mineral streak. Distinctive. High acid. 91/100

Nanclares y Prieto Oson Das Pisadas 2022 Rías Baixas, Spain
Mencia. Savoury, stony. Well balanced with restrained fruit. Has nice energy. 93/100

UK agent: https://www.indigowine.com/producer/alberto-nanclares/

title: Highlights: two vintages of the Blanc de Raisins Blanc from Mas de Daumas Gassac, Languedoc star
summary: Two versions of the same wine, eight years apart. Mas de Daumas Gassac are best known for their red wine,
content:
Two versions of the same wine, eight years apart. Mas de Daumas Gassac are best known for their red wine, but they grow a lot of interesting white varieties, and this is their top white, blending together an interesting mix of grapes. This ages so well.

Mas de Daumas Gassac Blanc de Raisins Blanc 2024 Languedoc, France
13.5% alcohol. 35% Viognier, 29% Chardonnay, 13% Petit Manseng, 10% Chenin, 6% Muscat, 7% others. Aromatic stone fruits with a hint of mint and dried herbs. The palate has aniseed, pear and white peach, as well as nice texture. So expressive, with some volume. 94/100

Mas de Daumas Gassac Blanc de Raisins Blanc 2016 Languedoc, France
14% alcohol. 27% Viognier, 25% Petit Manseng, 21% Chardonnay, 13% Chenin, 14% others. Bold and powerful. Richly textured with peach, pear and apricot fruit as well as some toast and spice notes and a lovely mineral dimension: it’s a sort of sweet textural minerality, finishing with mandarin and sea salt. This shows some evolution, but not as much as you’d think. Very stylish. 94/100
